_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Acute occlusions of lower extremity arteries and bypass grafts having salvageable limb ischemia.
Patients with viable limb (as evidenced by preserved distal capillary circulation and preserved venous return).
Patients with salvageable limb (as evidenced by preserved capillary circulation with absent venous drainage, but no motor loss).

preauth.label: Clinical Notes with planned line of treatment

claim.label: Post procedure colour doppler/Angio report of affected limb

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical Notes with planned line of treatment-detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure?
Is the colour doppler Report/Angio Report indicative of Peripheral Vascular Disease?

cpd_claim.q: Are detailed Procedure/ Operation notes submitted?
Does the Post procedure colour doppler/Angiogram of affected limb show recanalization of affected vessel?
Is detailed discharge summary with follow-up period submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes submitted for pre-authorization lack detailed history, signs & symptoms, or indication for procedure.
Doppler Report/Angiogram report submitted for pre-authorization is missing or not indicative of Peripheral Vascular Disease.
Procedure/operation notes submitted at claim stage are missing or lack necessary detail.
Post-procedure colour doppler/Angiogram report of affected limb is missing or does not show recanalization of affected vessel.
Invoices for catheter used or thrombolytic drug used are missing at claim submission.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Functional Cardiac Cath lab/ Interventional Radiology Lab
clinical_key_pointers: Acute limb ischemia (ALI) is potentially devastating but treatable presentation of peripheral arterial disease (PAD).
ALI may be caused by cardiac emboli, embolization from aneurysmal thrombus, and peripheral bypass graft thrombosis.
During arterial catheterization, iatrogenic causes may also lead to fresh thrombus formation.
The clinical signs and symptoms of acute limb ischemia include the six "P's”: pain, paralysis, paresthesias, pulselessness, pallor, and polar (i.e., a cold extremity).
The degree of symptoms suggests the underlying diagnosis.
Patients with embolic occlusion are often the most symptomatic and have an abrupt onset because collateral vessels are not developed to perfuse the limb in the face of occlusion.
surgical_options: Catheter directed thrombolysis has similar results as surgical intervention in carefully selected patients.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Decide referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY and selection of corresponding Health Benefit Package.
